output:
SBC Validator — guided walkthrough
  config vendor: audiocodes    ruleset: 2026-06-07

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 1 — INGEST  (raw vendor config → one normalized model)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  SBC FQDN: sbc-teams-01.contoso.com
  Teams leg:
      transport=tcp   mTLS=no   SRTP=no   keep-alive=no
      codecs=['PCMU', 'G722']   trusted roots in store: 5
  Carrier leg: transport=tls   keep-alive=yes   codecs=['PCMU', 'G722']
  Media realm 'Default': advertised=10.50.30.40   symmetric-RTP=no

  → Every check below reads THIS model, never the raw vendor syntax.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 2 — VALIDATE  (each domain reasons over the model)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  [A] syntax & semantic baseline (well-formed, no dangling refs)
        ✓ clean (or silent: nothing in the source to judge)
  [B] interop: TLS transport, OPTIONS keep-alive, header normalization
        [HIGH    ] B.SIP.TRANSPORT  —  Teams interface transport is 'tcp', not TLS
                  why: Direct Routing requires TLS for SIP signaling; TCP/UDP will be rejected by Teams.
                  fix: Set the Teams SIP interface transport to TLS.
        [MEDIUM  ] B.SIP.OPTIONS_KEEPALIVE  —  SIP OPTIONS keep-alive not enabled on Teams interface
                  why: Teams uses OPTIONS pings to track SBC health; without it the SBC can be marked down, causing intermittent call failures.
                  fix: Enable SIP OPTIONS keep-alive toward Teams.
  [C] the 2026 wedge: mTLS, the 7 required roots, SRTP, cert + chain
        [CRITICAL] C.CA.ROOT_MISSING  —  2 of 7 required root CAs missing
                  why: Trust store is missing root CAs Microsoft anchors Teams SIP certificates in (per the 2025-12 Direct Routing CA update). Handshake hard-fails once rotation reaches an untrusted root. Missing: DigiCert TLS ECC P384 Root G5 (SHA1 17F3DE5E9F0F19E98EF61F32266E20C407AE30EE); DigiCert TLS RSA 4096 Root G5 (SHA1 A78849DC5D7C758C8CDE399856B3AAD0B2A57135)
                  fix: Install the missing root CA chains into the Teams TLS context trust store before the next Microsoft rotation.
        [HIGH    ] C.TLS.MTLS_DISABLED  —  mTLS not enabled on Teams interface
                  why: Microsoft DR uses mutual TLS; without it the 2026 root-CA trust requirements aren't exercised and handshakes may fail.
                  fix: Enable mutual TLS on the Teams SIP TLS context.
        [HIGH    ] C.SRTP.DISABLED  —  SRTP not enabled on the Teams media leg
                  why: Microsoft Direct Routing requires encrypted media (SRTP, offered as SDP a=crypto). Without it Teams will not establish media and the call has no audio.
                  fix: Enable SRTP on the Teams leg with the AES_CM_128_HMAC_SHA1_80 crypto suite.
  [D] NAT / media: advertised address, symmetric RTP (one-way audio)
        [CRITICAL] D.NAT.PRIVATE_ADVERTISED  —  Non-routable IP advertised as public media address
                  why: Realm advertises non-routable IP 10.50.30.40 in SDP; remote-worker media will not return -> one-way audio.
                  fix: Set the advertised media address to a public, routable NAT IP.
        [MEDIUM  ] D.NAT.NO_SYMMETRIC_RTP  —  Symmetric RTP disabled
                  why: Without symmetric RTP/latching, media from NATed peers is often dropped, producing one-way or no audio.
                  fix: Enable symmetric RTP (latching) on the media realm.
  [E] codec: cross-leg overlap, transcode, DTMF
        [INFO    ] E.CODEC.AMRWB_NOT_OFFERED  —  Teams leg does not offer AMR-WB (2026 prioritized wideband)
                  why: Since 2026 Microsoft offers AMR-WB on Direct Routing trunks and prioritizes it when the SBC offers it back. Without it, calls negotiate another common codec (no outage), at reduced audio quality. Also confirm the SBC tolerates the unknown payload type 121 in Teams' SDP offer.
                  fix: Add AMR-WB to the Teams-leg codec list if the SBC supports it (HD audio); otherwise verify graceful fallback against the 2026 Teams SDP offer.
  [G] routing & classification (fires only if the source carries it)
        ✓ clean (or silent: nothing in the source to judge)
  [S] security: ACL default-deny, broad CIDR, IPv6, shadowing
        ✓ clean (or silent: nothing in the source to judge)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 3 — VERDICT  (severity-weighted; any CRITICAL forces BLOCK)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Risk score: 100/100      VERDICT: BLOCK
  Findings: CRITICAL 2, HIGH 3, MEDIUM 2, INFO 1

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 4 — PREDICT  (model the call: TLS → SIP → SDP → media)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Outcome: NO_CONNECT   (dies at: TLS handshake)
  Predicted: NO calls connect. Chain breaks at TLS handshake.

  SBC     Microsoft Teams SIP proxy
          -------------------------
            --> TLS ClientHello
            <-- TLS alert: unknown CA / handshake failed   << call dies here

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 5 — NEXT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Apply the fixes above to the SBC config, then re-run:
      sbc-validator validate <config> --ruleset <ruleset>
  to confirm it turns green. (See samples/walkthrough/ for a broken→fixed pair.)
